description: Naturalized Procedural Generation — realistic terrain, street networks, water systems, address coherence, vegetation, minimap/editor overhaul, export pipeline, and dynamic settlement evolution

userStories.title: Seeded noise module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a deterministic Perlin/Simplex noise implementation so that terrain generation is reproducible from the same seed across server and client.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/procedural/noise.ts with a seeded Simplex noise implementation
Function signature: createNoise2D(seed: string): (x: number, y: number) => number returning values in [-1, 1]
Add multi-octave fractal noise helper: fractalNoise(noise, x, y, octaves, lacunarity, persistence) => number
Same seed always produces identical output
Unit tests verify determinism: same seed = same values, different seeds = different values
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Basic heightmap generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a terrain generator that produces 2D heightmaps from noise so that settlements and worlds have elevation data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/generators/terrain-generator.ts with class TerrainGenerator
Method generateHeightmap(config: TerrainConfig): number[][] produces a normalized [0, 1] 2D array
TerrainConfig includes: seed, width, height, terrainType (plains/hills/mountains/coast/river/forest/desert), resolution (default 128)

Each terrain type has distinct noise parameters (amplitude, octaves, frequency): plains (amp 0.05, 3 oct), hills (0.15, 4 oct), mountains (0.40, 6 oct), coast (0.08, 3 oct), river (0.10, 4 oct), forest (0.08, 3 oct), desert (0.12, 2 oct)
Resolution scales by settlement type: 128 (village), 256 (town), 512 (city)
Uses the seeded noise module from US-001
Unit tests verify each terrain type produces height distributions within expected ranges
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Terrain feature stamping
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to stamp specific geographic features (peaks, valleys, canyons, cliffs, mesas) onto heightmaps so that terrain has recognizable landmarks.
acceptanceCriteria: Add methods to TerrainGenerator: stampPeak(), stampValley(), stampCanyon(), stampCliff(), stampMesa(), stampCrater()
Each method modifies the heightmap in-place at specified position with configurable radius and intensity
Peaks: Gaussian bump; Valleys: inverse Gaussian channel; Canyons: narrow deep cuts; Cliffs: step function; Mesas: flat-topped plateau; Craters: circular depression
Add stampFeatures(heightmap, terrainType, seed) that auto-places 2-5 features appropriate to the terrain type
Returns a TerrainFeature[] array with id, name, type, position, radius, elevation for each stamped feature
Feature names generated from a terrain-appropriate name list
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Slope and aspect map derivation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need slope and aspect maps derived from the heightmap so that building placement and road routing can check terrain steepness.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method deriveSlopeMap(heightmap: number[][]): number[][] to TerrainGenerator — returns gradient magnitude at each cell
Add method deriveAspectMap(heightmap: number[][]): number[][] — returns angle in radians of steepest descent
Add helper isBuildable(slopeMap, x, z, maxSlope = 0.52): boolean (0.52 rad = 30 degrees)
Add helper getRoadCost(slopeMap, x, z): number — returns traversal cost (1.0 flat, exponentially increasing, Infinity above ~15 degrees)
Unit tests verify: flat heightmap produces zero slope; a known ramp produces correct slope value
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Terrain schema and IR additions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema and IR types updated to store and export terrain data.
acceptanceCriteria: Add TerrainFeatureIR interface to shared/game-engine/ir-types.ts with fields: id, name, featureType (mountain/hill/valley/canyon/cliff/mesa/plateau/crater/ridge/pass), position: Vec3, radius: number, elevation: number, description: string | null
Add terrainFeatures: TerrainFeatureIR[] field to GeographyIR interface
Add slopeMap?: number[][] field to GeographyIR interface
Add to shared/schema.ts settlements table: elevation (integer), slopeProfile (text: flat/gentle/moderate/steep/terraced)
Create new terrain_features collection in mongo-storage.ts with CRUD operations: id, worldId, name, featureType, position, radius, elevation, description
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 3D terrain mesh rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Babylon.js game to render a terrain mesh from the heightmap so that the 3D world has visible elevation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/TerrainRenderer.ts with class TerrainRenderer
Method createTerrainMesh(heightmap: number[][], terrainSize: number, scene: Scene): Mesh generates a subdivided ground mesh with vertex Y positions from the heightmap
Apply biome-specific vertex coloring: green for low elevation, brown for mid, gray/white for high
Terrain mesh casts and receives shadows
Integrate into SettlementSceneManager.ts: when heightmap data is available, use TerrainRenderer instead of flat ground plane
Existing flat-ground fallback preserved when no heightmap data is present
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Street network data structures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared TypeScript types for the street network graph so that all generators and renderers use a consistent data model.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to shared/game-engine/types.ts: StreetNode interface with id, position: {x: number, z: number}, elevation: number, type (intersection/dead_end/T_junction/curve_point)

Add StreetEdge interface with id, name, fromNodeId, toNodeId, streetType (main_road/avenue/residential/alley/lane/boulevard/highway), width, waypoints: Vec3[], length, condition, traffic, sidewalks: boolean, hasStreetLights: boolean
Add StreetNetwork interface with nodes: StreetNode[], edges: StreetEdge[]
Add StreetNetworkIR, StreetNodeIR, StreetEdgeIR to shared/game-engine/ir-types.ts
Update SettlementIR to include optional streetNetwork: StreetNetworkIR
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Street generator core with organic pattern
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a street generator that produces connected street network graphs, starting with the organic/medieval layout pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/generators/street-generator.ts with class StreetGenerator
Method generateOrganic(config: StreetGenConfig): StreetNetwork generates a connected graph
StreetGenConfig includes: center: {x, z}, radius: number, settlementType, seed, slopeMap (optional)
Algorithm: start from center, generate 3-5 main roads radiating at irregular angles, branch secondary roads at varied angles, add tertiary curved connections
Apply Perlin noise displacement to waypoints for organic curves
If slopeMap provided, roads avoid high-slope cells via cost-weighted pathfinding
All edges have non-zero length, valid from/to node references, and at least 2 waypoints
Generated graph is connected (all nodes reachable from any other)
Unit test: generates a valid connected graph with expected node/edge count range for a village
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Grid street pattern
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a grid-based street layout pattern for planned towns and cities.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method generateGrid(config: StreetGenConfig): StreetNetwork to StreetGenerator
Generates an N x M block grid where N and M scale with settlement radius
Perturb grid intersection positions with low-amplitude noise for imperfection (+/-2-5% of block size)
Optionally add 1-2 diagonal avenues for cities with population > 3000
Main streets (every Nth row/column) are wider boulevard/avenue type; others are residential
Grid axis optionally rotated to align with a provided direction vector
All edges have correct from/to references and graph is connected
Unit test verifies grid structure and connectivity
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Radial street pattern
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a radial/concentric street layout for capital cities and fortress towns.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method generateRadial(config: StreetGenConfig): StreetNetwork to StreetGenerator
Central plaza node with 6-8 radial boulevard edges emanating outward
2-4 concentric ring road edges at increasing radii connecting the radials
Radial roads may curve slightly with noise-based lateral displacement
Inner ring edges are boulevard type; outer ring edges are residential
All intersections properly connected (radial x ring creates intersection nodes)
Unit test verifies radial structure and connectivity
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Linear street pattern
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a linear street layout for river towns, mining towns, and highway stops.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method generateLinear(config: StreetGenConfig & { axis: Vec2 }): StreetNetwork to StreetGenerator
One main street running along the provided axis direction through settlement center
Short perpendicular side streets branching off at semi-regular intervals
Main street is main_road type; side streets are residential or lane
Side streets alternate left/right or appear on both sides
Optional: main street curves to follow a provided path (river centerline)
Unit test verifies linear topology
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Hillside terraced street pattern
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a terraced/switchback street layout for mountain settlements where roads follow elevation contour lines.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method generateHillside(config: StreetGenConfig, heightmap: number[][]): StreetNetwork to StreetGenerator
Generate contour-following roads at 3-5 elevation levels within settlement radius
Connect contour roads with switchback ramps (zigzag segments going uphill)
Contour roads are residential type; switchback connectors are lane type
Requires heightmap input; falls back to organic pattern if not provided
Unit test with a known sloped heightmap verifies terrace structure
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Waterfront street pattern
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a waterfront street layout for port towns and lakeside villages.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method generateWaterfront(config: StreetGenConfig & { shorelinePoints: Vec2[] }): StreetNetwork to StreetGenerator
Main road follows the provided shoreline curve offset inland by a configurable distance
Perpendicular roads extend inland from the waterfront road at regular intervals
A secondary parallel road runs further inland for larger settlements
Waterfront road is boulevard type; perpendicular roads are residential
Dock/pier stub nodes extend seaward from waterfront road between perpendicular streets
Unit test with a mock shoreline verifies waterfront topology
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Street pattern selection logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automatic selection of the appropriate street pattern based on settlement attributes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add function selectStreetPattern(config: GeographyConfig): StreetPatternType to street-generator.ts

Selection rules: coast -> waterfront; river -> linear; mountains + steep -> hillside; city + large pop -> grid; city + capital -> radial; village -> organic; older settlements (foundedYear < 1800) -> organic; newer -> grid
Add public method generate(config, geographyConfig, heightmap?) that calls selectStreetPattern and dispatches to correct algorithm
Pattern type stored on settlement record for reference
Unit tests verify correct pattern selection for each terrain/type combination
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Street naming system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a contextual street naming system that assigns unique, appropriate names to streets.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method assignStreetNames(network: StreetNetwork, seed: string): void to StreetGenerator that mutates edge names in-place
Street type determines suffix: boulevards/avenues for main arteries, streets for through-roads, drives/ways for curved residential, lanes/courts for dead-ends, alleys for narrow passages
Grid layouts use numbered cross-streets (1st St, 2nd St) for one axis and named streets for the other
Named streets draw from expandable name pools: tree names, landmark references, geographic terms, historical figures
No two streets in the same settlement share the same name
Continuous street segments (multiple edges forming one road) share the same name
Unit tests verify uniqueness and suffix-type correspondence
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integrate street generator into geography generator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the geography generator to use the new street generator instead of its current random street placement.
acceptanceCriteria: Refactor geography-generator.ts: replace generateStreets() with a call to StreetGenerator.generate()
New generation flow: generateStreetNetwork() -> deriveDistricts() -> generateBuildings()
Districts derived from street regions using Voronoi-like clustering around seed points bounded by major streets
Buildings generated along street edges (evenly spaced positions — proper lot placement comes in US-018)
Settlement streets field in DB stores the StreetNetwork object as JSONB
Existing settlement data still loads without errors (backward compat)
Integration test: GeographyGenerator.generate() produces settlement with connected street network, unique names, buildings assigned to streets
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Road renderer update for street networks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Babylon.js road renderer to render intra-settlement streets from the street network graph.
acceptanceCriteria: Update RoadGenerator.ts: add method generateStreetNetworkRoads(network: StreetNetworkIR, sampleHeight) that renders each edge as a ribbon mesh
Road width varies by street type: boulevard=6, avenue=5, main_road=4, residential=3, lane=2, alley=1.5
Road material/color varies by street type: main roads darker, residential lighter, alleys dirt-colored
SettlementSceneManager.ts updated to call new method when street network data is present
Existing MST-based inter-settlement road generation preserved
Fallback: if no street network data, use existing radial road generation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Street-frontage lot placement
userStories.description: As a developer, I need lots placed along street edges with proper frontage so that buildings line streets realistically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create function generateLotsAlongStreets(network: StreetNetwork, config: GeographyConfig): LotPosition[] in lot-generator.ts or street-generator.ts
LotPosition includes: position: Vec3, facingAngle: number, width: number, depth: number, side: left/right, distanceAlongStreet: number, streetEdgeId: string
Walk along each street edge placing lots on both sides with configurable lot width (village=15, town=12, city=10) and setback
Lots do not overlap each other or the street
Lot facing angle is perpendicular to street direction at lot position (facing toward street)
Total lot count approximately matches config.population / 4
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Address assignment algorithm
userStories.description: As a developer, I need house numbers assigned based on position along the street so that addresses are spatially coherent.
acceptanceCriteria: Create function assignAddresses(lots: LotPosition[], network: StreetNetwork, centerPoint: Vec2): void that sets houseNumber, streetName, and address on each lot
Left side of street (relative to direction away from center) gets odd numbers; right side gets even
Numbers increase monotonically moving away from settlement center along each street
In grid layouts, use block-based numbering: 100s for first block, 200s for second, etc.
Number gaps proportional to physical distance between lots
Corner lots get address of higher-priority street they face
Full address: houseNumber + streetName (e.g. 42 Oak Ave)
Unit test: verify odd/even sides, monotonic increase, no duplicates within settlement
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Block structure generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need blocks defined as enclosed regions bounded by streets for navigable city blocks with address ranges.
acceptanceCriteria: Create function generateBlocks(network: StreetNetwork): Block[] that finds enclosed polygons in street graph
Block interface: id, boundaryStreetIds: string[], polygon: Vec2[], districtId: string, blockNumber: number, center: Vec3
Use minimal cycle detection on the planar street graph to find enclosed faces
Each lot assigned to the block whose polygon contains it
Block numbers increase outward from settlement center (100, 200, 300...)
Add Block and BlockIR types to shared/game-engine/types.ts and ir-types.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Lot schema updates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the lots database table updated with new fields so that street-aligned lot data persists correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fields to lots table in shared/schema.ts: lotWidth (number), lotDepth (number), streetEdgeId (text), distanceAlongStreet (number), side (text: left/right), blockId (text), facingAngle (number), elevation (number), foundationType (text: flat/raised/stilted/terraced)
Update LotIR in ir-types.ts with matching fields
Update mongo-storage.ts Lot Mongoose schema to include new fields
Update persistLotsAndBuildings() in geography-generator.ts to write new fields when generating lots
Existing lots without new fields default to sensible values (lotWidth=12, lotDepth=16, side=left, facingAngle=0, elevation=0, foundationType=flat)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Address validation pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an address validation step after generation to catch and fix inconsistencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Create function validateAddresses(lots, streetNetwork): ValidationResult in geography generator or dedicated module
ValidationResult includes: valid: boolean, errors: string[], warnings: string[], fixesApplied: string[]
Checks: no duplicate addresses within settlement; house numbers monotonically increasing per street; all lots reference valid street edge; street names match assigned edge
Auto-fix: renumber duplicates by appending suffix (e.g. 42A Oak Ave) and log the fix
Validation runs automatically at end of GeographyGenerator.generate()
Errors logged to console with settlement ID
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Settlement boundary generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need terrain-aware settlement boundaries so settlements have irregular shapes conforming to geography.
acceptanceCriteria: Create function generateSettlementBoundary(config: GeographyConfig, heightmap?: number[][]): SettlementBoundary
SettlementBoundary interface: polygon: Vec2[], area: number, perimeterType: natural/walled/open
Plains/forest: roughly circular with Perlin noise perturbation on radius
Coast: semi-circular, one side follows coastline
Mountains: elongated, confined to lower-slope areas
River: elongated along river axis
Desert: compact polygon centered on water source
Store boundary polygon on settlement record as JSONB field boundaryPolygon
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Terrain-influenced district placement
userStories.description: As a developer, I need districts placed according to terrain suitability so that commercial districts occupy flat areas and wealthy districts get overlooks.
acceptanceCriteria: Refactor district generation to accept heightmap and slope map
Commercial/market district placed at flattest most central accessible area (lowest average slope near center)
Wealthy residential placed at moderate elevation with good views
Working-class residential placed in lower-lying or less desirable terrain
Industrial district placed near water features or settlement periphery
Religious/civic district placed at highest accessible point within settlement
Falls back to current radial placement when no heightmap available
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Settlement subtype system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need expanded settlement subtypes so generation can produce distinct forms like port cities and mountain villages.
acceptanceCriteria: Add settlementSubtype field (text) to settlements table in shared/schema.ts

Define subtypes: port_city, mountain_village, river_crossing, mining_town, fortress_town, oasis_settlement, fishing_village, crossroads_town, university_town, market_town, cliff_dwelling, island_settlement, valley_town, standard
Add function inferSettlementSubtype(config: GeographyConfig): string that selects subtype based on terrain, population, settlement type
Each subtype has SubtypeConfig with: required landmark types, preferred street pattern, building style hints, special features
Subtype auto-assigned during generation and stored on settlement record
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Elevation-aware building placement
userStories.description: As a developer, I need buildings placed with terrain-adaptive foundations so buildings on slopes don't float or clip.
acceptanceCriteria: Create function calculateFoundation(lotPosition: Vec3, lotSize, heightmap: number[][]): FoundationData
FoundationData interface: type (flat/raised/stilted/terraced), baseElevation: number, foundationHeight: number, retainingWall: boolean
Logic: sample 4 corner elevations; delta < 0.3 -> flat; < 1.0 -> raised; < 2.5 -> stilted; else terraced with retaining wall
Store foundationType and elevation on each lot record
ProceduralBuildingGenerator.ts updated to add foundation mesh beneath buildings where foundationHeight > 0
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Settlement elevation profile
userStories.description: As a developer, I need settlement-level elevation metadata so maps and UIs can display elevation information.
acceptanceCriteria: Create function calculateElevationProfile(heightmap, boundary): SettlementElevation
SettlementElevation includes: minElevation, maxElevation, averageElevation, slopeProfile (flat/gentle/moderate/steep/terraced)
slopeProfile derived from average slope: < 5 deg flat, < 15 gentle, < 25 moderate, < 35 steep, else terraced
Generate contour lines at configurable intervals (default every 10% of elevation range): array of {elevation, points: Vec2[]}
Store elevation, slopeProfile, and contourLines (JSONB) on settlement record
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: River generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need procedural river generation that carves realistic waterways through terrain.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/generators/water-generator.ts with class WaterGenerator
Method generateRivers(heightmap, config: {seed, count, terrainType}): RiverData[] produces 1-3 rivers
Algorithm: place sources at high-elevation points, simulate flow following steepest descent with momentum, accumulate width downstream, terminate at sea level or map edge
Apply meander displacement (sine wave perpendicular to flow, amplitude increases on flatter terrain)
Carve river channel into heightmap (lower elevation along path proportional to flow volume)
RiverData includes: id, name, source: Vec3, mouth: Vec3, waypoints: Vec3[], widthProfile: number[], flowDirection
River names generated from name pool
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: River crossing points
userStories.description: As a developer, I need river crossing points (bridges, fords) generated where streets intersect rivers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method generateCrossings(rivers, streetNetwork): RiverCrossingData[] to WaterGenerator
For each street edge crossing a river (line-segment intersection test), generate a crossing point
Crossing type by river width: < 5 -> ford; < 15 -> bridge; >= 15 -> large bridge or ferry
RiverCrossingData includes: position: Vec3, type (bridge/ford/ferry), streetEdgeId, riverId, name
Crossing names follow pattern: StreetName Bridge or RiverName Ford
Store crossings on river data and add crossingPoints field to RiverIR
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Lake generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need procedural lake generation from terrain basins.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method generateLakes(heightmap, config): LakeData[] to WaterGenerator
Algorithm: find local minima (basins), fill to lowest overflow point, surface = flat at pour point elevation, boundary = contour at pour point
LakeData includes: id, name, center: Vec3, elevation, boundaryPolygon: Vec2[], area, maxDepth
Generate 0-3 lakes depending on terrain type (more in forest/hills, none in desert unless oasis)
Lake names from pool
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Coastline and bay generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need coastline and bay shapes for coastal settlements.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method generateCoastline(heightmap, seaLevel): CoastlineData to WaterGenerator
Coastline extracted as contour at sea level with noise perturbation
Classify segments by slope: gentle -> beach; steep -> cliff; moderate -> rocky
Add method generateBays(coastline, seed): BayData[] that stamps 0-2 concave indentations
CoastlineData includes segments array with points and type (beach/cliff/rocky/marsh)
BayData includes id, name, center, mouthWidth, depth, boundaryPolygon, harborSuitability (0-1)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Harbor and dock generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need harbors and docks generated for coastal and river settlements.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method generateHarbor(settlement, coastline, bays): HarborData | null to WaterGenerator
Harbor placed in most sheltered bay (highest harborSuitability) or calmest coastline segment
Generate 2-6 docks: DockData with position, length, width, rotation, dockType (fishing/trade/ferry/military)
Optionally place lighthouse at harbor entrance or nearby promontory
HarborData includes id, name, position, docks, lighthousePosition, waterDepth, shelterRating
Only generated for settlements with terrain coast or river and population > 50
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Water feature schema and IR types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need schema and IR types for all water features so they persist and export correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to ir-types.ts: RiverIR, RiverCrossingIR, LakeIR, CoastlineIR, CoastSegmentIR, BayIR, HarborIR, DockIR interfaces per roadmap specs
Add to GeographyIR: rivers: RiverIR[], lakes: LakeIR[], coastline: CoastlineIR | null, bays: BayIR[], harbors: HarborIR[], seaLevel: number
Add to settlements schema: waterFeatures (jsonb array), hasHarbor (boolean), harborData (jsonb)
Create water_features collection in mongo-storage.ts: id, worldId, featureType (river/lake/bay/coastline), name, data (jsonb)
Add CRUD operations for water features
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Water rendering in 3D
userStories.description: As a developer, I need water features rendered in the Babylon.js 3D engine.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/WaterRenderer.ts with class WaterRenderer
Method createRiverMesh(river: RiverIR, scene): Mesh generates ribbon mesh following waypoints with width from widthProfile at correct elevation
Method createLakeMesh(lake: LakeIR, scene): Mesh generates flat plane clipped to boundary polygon at lake elevation
Method createOceanMesh(seaLevel, terrainSize, scene): Mesh generates large water plane at sea level
Water material: semi-transparent blue with subtle animated UV offset for flow (rivers) or wave (lakes/ocean)
Method createBridgeMesh(crossing: RiverCrossingIR, scene): Mesh generates simple arch or flat bridge
Integrate into SettlementSceneManager.ts: render water when data present
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Street-facing building orientation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need buildings oriented to face their assigned street.
acceptanceCriteria: Update ProceduralBuildingGenerator.ts: when lot has facingAngle set, use it as building Y-axis rotation
facingAngle calculated perpendicular to street direction at lot position, facing toward street
Add +/-3 degree random rotation variance (seeded) for natural imperfection
Update BuildingIR.rotation to use lot facingAngle in ir-generator.ts
Buildings without facingAngle fall back to existing rotation logic
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Corner building handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need corner buildings at intersections handled specially so they face the correct street.
acceptanceCriteria: In lot generation, identify corner lots: lots within one lot width of a node connecting to another street
Corner lots face the higher-priority street (by streetType hierarchy: boulevard > avenue > main_road > residential > lane > alley)
If equal priority, face the one with more frontage
Mark corner lots with isCorner boolean flag in LotPosition
Corner lots optionally have 1.2x width for more prominent buildings
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Building scale by zone
userStories.description: As a developer, I need building size and style to vary by urban zone.
acceptanceCriteria: Create function getZoneConfig(distanceFromCenter, settlementRadius, settlementType): ZoneConfig
ZoneConfig includes: zoneName, lotWidth, lotDepth, maxFloors, setback, sideGap
Downtown: lots 8x10, 2-5 floors, 0 setback, 0 side gap (shared walls)
Mixed use: lots 10x14, 2-3 floors, 1m setback
Inner residential: lots 12x16, 1-2 floors, 2m setback
Outer residential: lots 15x25, 1-2 floors, 4m setback
Rural edge: lots 25x40, 1 floor, 8m setback
Update lot generation to use zone-based dimensions; update building generation to use zone-based floor counts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Terrain-adaptive foundation rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I need visible foundation meshes beneath buildings on slopes.
acceptanceCriteria: In ProceduralBuildingGenerator.ts, check foundationType and foundationHeight from lot data
flat: no additional geometry (current behavior)
raised: add box mesh beneath building matching footprint with height = foundationHeight, stone/concrete material
stilted: add 4 cylinder meshes (stilts) at corners with height = foundationHeight
terraced: add retaining wall mesh (thin box) on downhill side of building
Foundation meshes use neutral stone/concrete material color
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Minimap terrain background layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the minimap to display terrain coloring from the heightmap.
acceptanceCriteria: Update BabylonMinimap.ts: add terrain background rendering method
Render colored image onto minimap background based on heightmap: green low, brown mid, gray/white high
Overlay biome-specific tinting (forest=darker green, desert=tan, coast=blue at sea level)
Water features (rivers, lakes) rendered as blue pixels/lines on minimap background
Terrain background updates when player moves to new settlement or region
Falls back to current solid dark background when no heightmap data available
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Minimap street network layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the minimap to display street lines.
acceptanceCriteria: Add street rendering layer to BabylonMinimap.ts on top of terrain background
Each street edge rendered as line transformed from world to minimap pixel coordinates
Line width proportional to street type (main roads = 2px, residential = 1px, alleys = hairline)
Line color: light gray for residential, white for main roads
Only rendered when street network data available for current settlement
Performance: street lines pre-rendered to cached texture, updated only on settlement change
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Minimap building footprint layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need building footprints on the minimap at higher zoom levels.
acceptanceCriteria: Add building rendering layer to BabylonMinimap.ts
Each building rendered as small rectangle (2-3px) at lot position rotated by facingAngle
Color-coded: residential = light green, commercial = light blue, civic/landmark = yellow
Only rendered when minimap zoom level >= 2x to avoid clutter
Existing dot markers (player, NPC, quest) render on top of building layer
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Minimap zoom and interaction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the minimap to support zoom and basic interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Add zoom support to BabylonMinimap.ts: zoom in/out via mouse wheel or GUI +/- buttons
Zoom range: 1x (default, shows full settlement) to 8x (close detail)
Minimap view centers on player position and pans as player moves
Add north indicator (small N or arrow) in corner of minimap
Add compass rose or direction indicator
Zoom level persists within session but resets to 1x on settlement change
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Full-screen map view
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a full-screen 2D map overlay for detailed settlement/world viewing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/FullScreenMap.ts using Babylon.js GUI AdvancedDynamicTexture
Toggle with configurable key (default M) or button
Shows 2D top-down view: terrain coloring, street network with name labels, building footprints with business/landmark labels, player position and facing, quest markers
Pan by dragging and zoom with scroll wheel
Close with same key or Escape
Performance: render to cached canvas, update only on data change

userStories.title: Poisson disc sampling module

acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/procedural/poisson-disc.ts with function poissonDiscSampling(width, height, minDistance, seed, maxAttempts = 30): Vec2[]
Implements Bridson's algorithm for O(n) Poisson disc sampling
Uses seeded random for deterministic output
Returns array of 2D positions with guaranteed minimum distance between any two points
Unit test: all returned points have pairwise distance >= minDistance; output deterministic for same seed

Elevation bands: 0-0.15 hot, 0.15-0.35 temperate, 0.35-0.55 cool, 0.55-0.75 cold, 0.75+ frozen/barren
Moisture from distance to nearest water: < 30 units wet, 30-100 moderate, > 100 dry
Cross-reference elevation x moisture for zone selection (e.g. hot+wet = tropical forest, cold+dry = bare rock)

Place ground cover (shrubs, grass, flowers) between trees using secondary finer Poisson disc pass

userStories.title: Nature object LOD system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need level-of-detail rendering for nature objects for performance.
acceptanceCriteria: Update ProceduralNatureGenerator.ts with LOD-aware rendering
Near (< 50 units): full 3D mesh with shadows
Medium (50-150 units): simplified mesh, no shadows
Far (150-400 units): billboard quad (camera-facing plane with texture)
Very far (> 400 units): culled (not rendered)
LOD transitions happen smoothly per frame based on camera distance
LOD distance thresholds configurable via constructor parameter
Performance: at least 30fps with 1000+ nature objects in view
Typecheck passes
